observed_application_pressure: Model cards emphasize intended use, limitations, and subgroup performance reporting.
claim_governance_gap: A model card can document limits, but a reviewer still needs to decide whether a specific metric sentence is licensed or overextended.
example_submitted_claim: The model is reliable for all intended users.
licensed_response: Rewrite to a subgroup/context-bounded claim tied to the reported evaluation slice.
tool_mapping: reviewer_claim_intake: rewrite forbidden broad model claim to licensed local claim
boundary: not a replacement for full model cards

observed_application_pressure: Datasheets document dataset motivation, composition, collection, recommended uses, and related context.
claim_governance_gap: Dataset documentation does not by itself decide whether a downstream empirical claim crosses source or use boundaries.
example_submitted_claim: This dataset validates deployment in the target domain.
licensed_response: Reject admission until evidence unit, G/Q/U, action, preorder, forbidden claim, and visual anchor are declared.
tool_mapping: template_admission: require evidence unit plus G/Q/U and forbidden claim before domain admission
boundary: not a raw-data redistribution or dataset-quality guarantee

observed_application_pressure: WILDS collects datasets with natural distribution shifts across settings such as hospitals, camera traps, location, and time.
claim_governance_gap: A benchmark score under shift can be misread as a broader deployment or cross-context robustness claim.
example_submitted_claim: The method is robust to real-world shifts.
licensed_response: Rewrite to the specific shift family, metric layer, and decision-use boundary evidenced by the benchmark cell.
tool_mapping: claim_audit_report: expose G boundary and decision-use limits before emitting a claim

observed_application_pressure: CheckList turns model evaluation into targeted capability and behavior tests rather than only aggregate accuracy.
claim_governance_gap: Behavioral failures still need claim-level wording discipline: a failed capability test licenses a bounded behavior claim, not universal model invalidity.
example_submitted_claim: The model does not understand negation.
licensed_response: Weaken to a diagnostic claim about the tested capability and generated test family.
tool_mapping: reviewer_claim_intake: rewrite tempting global claim to bounded diagnostic claim

observed_application_pressure: Robustness Gym unifies subpopulations, transformations, evaluation sets, and adversarial attacks for NLP evaluation.
claim_governance_gap: A toolkit can produce many slices and tests; the reporting layer must still prevent slice evidence from becoming broad robustness language.
example_submitted_claim: The model is robust after passing the evaluated slices.
licensed_response: Admit only as a slice/test-family claim or support-only robustness witness unless the template contract licenses broader language.
tool_mapping: claim_template_admission: require template role and support-only ceiling before adding new evaluation rows

observed_application_pressure: Selective classification trades coverage for risk and supports abstention or reject-option reasoning.
claim_governance_gap: A low-risk selected subset claim should not automatically become a full-coverage performance or deployment claim.
example_submitted_claim: The classifier has low deployment error.
licensed_response: Rewrite to risk-at-coverage over the selected subset, or suppress the full-coverage deployment claim.
tool_mapping: reviewer_claim_intake: suppress or rewrite unsupported full-coverage claim under declared use context
